page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 180 dedicated vCPUs derived from 90 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor, running two threads per core. The system is equipped with 1440.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Cache resources include 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 caches. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top tier, with read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching 13485.99 GB/sec and large block read/write bandwidths of 349.44 GB/sec and 177.32 GB/sec respectively. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (F, VNNI, and BF16) and cryptography acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI), which contribute to top-tier scores in Passmark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests.

While single-core performance is strong (top 25%), certain single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression perform at average levels (middle 50%). However, the instance excels in memory-intensive and highly parallelized workloads. It achieves top-tier performance in Redis SET operations (210.86 million operations/sec) and software compilation. Additionally, despite the lack of dedicated GPUs, the high core count and AVX-512 support enable top-tier LLM inference speeds for medium (7B) and large (70B) models. This profile is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy compilation pipelines, and memory-bound scientific simulations.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 180 dedicated vCPUs and 1440.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 90 physical cores and a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and VAES, enabling top-tier results in cryptographic and extended instruction benchmarks. While single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels, the instance excels in memory-intensive operations, achieving top-tier throughput in Redis SET operations and software compilation. Lacking local storage and GPUs, its hardware profile is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, parallelized data processing, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized instance with 180 vCPUs and 1440 GB RAM. Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14, it delivers top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server with 180 vCPUs and 1440 GB RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring 180 vCPUs and 1.4 TB of RAM for demanding database and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: 180 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors
1440 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU performance
Advanced vector and cryptographic instruction support (AVX-512, VAES)
Suited for in-memory databases, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
